POs.name: Engineering knowledge
POs.desc: Apply knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals and specialisation to solve complex engineering problems.

POs.name: Problem analysis
POs.desc: Identify, formulate, review research literature and analyse complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles.

POs.name: Design/develop solutions
POs.desc: Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components that meet specified needs with appropriate consideration for public health, safety, culture and environment.

POs.name: Conduct investigations
POs.desc: Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data to provide valid conclusions.

POs.name: Modern tool usage
POs.desc: Create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources and modern engineering and IT tools to complex engineering activities.

POs.name: Engineer & society
POs.desc: Apply reasoning informed by contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and consequent responsibilities relevant to professional engineering practice.

POs.name: Environment & sustainability
POs.desc: Understand the impact of the professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for, sustainable development.

POs.name: Ethics
POs.desc: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of engineering practice.

POs.name: Individual & team work
POs.desc: Function effectively as an individual and as a member or leader in diverse teams and in multidisciplinary settings.

POs.name: Communication
POs.desc: Communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large, including writing effective reports and design documentation.

POs.name: Project management & finance
POs.desc: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of engineering and management principles and apply these to one's own work, as a member or leader in a team.

CSE101.name: Problem Solving & Programming
CSE101.description: Introduces structured programming using Python and problem-solving techniques.

units.title: Problem solving and algorithmic thinking
units.co: Apply structured problem-solving techniques to decompose real-world problems into algorithmic steps

topics.s: Problem definition, problem-solving steps, characteristics of a good algorithm
Notation, symbols, flowchart design for sequential, conditional and iterative problems
Writing pseudocode, converting flowcharts to pseudocode, common conventions
Concept of time and space complexity, Big-O notation informally
Breaking down problems into sub-problems, top-down design, worked examples

teaching.d: Instructor solves 2–3 problems live on board using algorithmic steps
Students attempt flowchart for a given problem individually, then compare with a peer
VisuAlgo to show algorithm execution step-by-step
Design algorithms and flowcharts for 5 real-world problems of increasing complexity

assessment.d: Identify correct flowchart symbols, trace algorithm output
Submit 5 flowcharts + pseudocode solutions with complexity annotations
Explain algorithmic approach for a given problem verbally

refs.ti: Problem Solving and Program Design in C
NPTEL — Programming, Data Structures and Algorithms

units.title: Python fundamentals — syntax, control flow and functions
units.co: Write, test and debug Python programs using control structures, functions and modules

topics.s: Installing Python, IDLE vs VS Code, running scripts, interactive mode
int, float, str, bool; arithmetic, relational, logical operators; type conversion
if-elif-else, nested conditions; while loop, for loop, range(); break, continue, pass
def keyword, parameters vs arguments, return values, default args, scope and namespaces
Reading tracebacks, common errors, print-based debugging, basic assertions

teaching.d: Instructor writes and runs code in real time; students follow along on their own machines
Students alternate driver/navigator roles for lab exercises
Students are given buggy programs and must identify and fix all errors
Short video on functions assigned before class; class time used for problem-solving

assessment.d: 2–3 coding tasks per lab session, auto-graded or peer-reviewed
Students review a peer's function implementation and provide structured feedback
Trace and write Python code using control flow and functions
Fill-in output questions on operators, loops and scope

refs.ti: Python Programming: An Introduction to Computer Science
Automate the Boring Stuff with Python

refs.au: Dr. Chuck Severance, University of Michigan via Coursera

units.title: Data handling — lists, dictionaries and file I/O
units.co: Implement basic data handling using lists, dictionaries and file I/O in Python

topics.s: Creation, indexing, slicing, list methods, list comprehensions, tuples vs lists
String methods, slicing, formatting with f-strings, string as iterable
Key-value pairs, CRUD operations, nested dicts, sets and set operations
Opening/closing files, read/write/append modes, reading line by line, with statement
csv module, parsing real-world datasets, basic data cleaning

teaching.d: Students work with a real CSV to practise file I/O and dictionaries
Instructor builds a contact book application live demonstrating dictionaries and file persistence
Teams compete to solve a data manipulation problem using lists and dicts
Students begin their mini project — reading and processing a dataset of their choice

assessment.d: Implement a student record system using dicts and file I/O
Data loading and basic analysis on chosen dataset
Predict output, write code, trace dictionary operations
Explain data structure choices and file handling approach

refs.ti: Python Programming: An Introduction to Computer Science
NPTEL — The Joy of Computing using Python

units.title: Algorithm efficiency and complexity analysis
units.co: Analyse the efficiency of simple algorithms using time and space complexity concepts

topics.s: O, Ω, Θ notation; best, worst and average case; rules for computing Big-O
Single and nested loops, linear and quadratic complexity, common patterns
Linear search O(n), binary search O(log n) — implementation, comparison, preconditions
Bubble sort, selection sort, insertion sort — implementation, trace and complexity; introduction to merge sort
Base case and recursive case, call stack, factorial, Fibonacci, recursive vs iterative trade-offs

teaching.d: Use sorting visualisers (visualgo.net) to demonstrate algorithm behaviour step-by-step
Students derive Big-O for 10 code snippets in timed pairs activity
Students implement and benchmark bubble sort vs merge sort on varying dataset sizes
Instructor guides students to derive the optimal approach through questions

assessment.d: Derive Big-O for 15 given code segments
Implement 3 sorting algorithms, measure runtime, plot results
Trace recursion, write algorithm, analyse complexity
Compare two algorithms on a real problem and justify with complexity analysis

refs.ti: Data Structures and Algorithm Analysis in Python
refs.au: M.T. Goodrich, R. Tamassia & M.H. Goldwasser

units.title: Professional coding practices, OOP basics and version control
units.co: Demonstrate professional coding practices including documentation and version control basics

topics.s: PEP 8 guidelines, naming conventions, indentation, line length, meaningful identifiers
Inline comments, docstrings (Google/NumPy style), auto-generated docs with pydoc, README files
Classes and objects, __init__, instance and class variables, methods, encapsulation concept
git init, add, commit, status, log; branching; pushing to GitHub
Project finalisation, README writing, GitHub repository submission, demo and peer review

teaching.d: Students review and improve a poorly documented codebase using PEP 8 and docstring standards
Each student creates a GitHub repo, makes commits, resolves a merge conflict in pairs
Instructor builds a BankAccount class live; students extend it with new methods
Students present their Sem 1 mini project to peers — 5 min demo + Q&A

assessment.d: Refactor a given messy Python file — PEP 8, docstrings, naming
Final mini project on GitHub with README, commit history, documented code
OOP concepts, write a class, Git command sequence
Live demo and verbal Q&A on mini project

refs.ti: Clean Code: A Handbook of Agile Software Craftsmanship
CS50P — Introduction to Programming with Python

units.title: Differential calculus and applications
units.co: Apply concepts of differential and integral calculus to solve engineering problems

topics.s: Definition of limits, L'Hôpital's rule, continuity and types of discontinuity
Rules of differentiation — product, quotient, chain rule; higher-order derivatives
Maxima and minima, curve sketching, rate of change, tangent and normal
Expansion of functions, convergence, approximation of sin x, cos x, eˣ
Partial derivatives, Euler's theorem for homogeneous functions, total derivatives

teaching.d: Step-by-step derivation of key theorems with geometric interpretation on board
Weekly tutorials where students solve 5–8 problems in supervised groups
Relate derivatives to signal slopes, velocity, heat transfer rates
Students exchange and solve each other's problems then verify solutions

assessment.d: 10 problems on differentiation and applications submitted weekly
MCQ on Taylor series expansion and partial derivatives
Short in-class test on curve tracing and applications

units.title: Integral calculus and vector calculus
units.co: Use matrix operations and linear transformations in computational problem solving

topics.s: Standard forms, integration by parts, substitution, partial fractions
Convergence, Beta and Gamma functions and their properties
Double and triple integrals, change of order of integration, Jacobian
Del operator, physical interpretation, Laplacian
Green's theorem, Stokes' theorem, Gauss divergence theorem

teaching.d: Intuition before formula — explain what an integral represents geometrically before computing
Prove Green's and Stokes' theorem with clear step-by-step derivation
Area under curve, volume of solid, fluid flow using integrals
Students watch NPTEL lecture on multiple integrals before tutorial

assessment.d: Evaluate 15 integrals covering all techniques — improper, multiple, Beta-Gamma
Definite integrals, double integrals and vector calculus
Identify correct theorem to apply — Green's, Stokes', Gauss
Student presents and explains solution to one vector calculus problem

units.title: Matrices and linear algebra
units.co: Evaluate limits, continuity and differentiability in functions of one and several variables

topics.s: Types of matrices, matrix operations, transpose, inverse, adjoint
Properties of determinants, cofactor expansion, Cramer's rule
Gaussian elimination, rank, consistency, unique and infinite solutions
Characteristic equation, diagonalisation, Cayley-Hamilton theorem
Kernel, range, null space, rank-nullity theorem, matrix representation

teaching.d: Solve full systems using Gaussian elimination, trace eigenvalue computation step by step
Use MATLAB or Python (NumPy) to verify hand-computed matrix results
Relate eigenvalues to Google PageRank, PCA in data science, vibration analysis
Students work in groups of 3 to solve a linear system with real engineering data

assessment.d: Solve 3 linear systems, compute eigenvalues of 3×3 matrices — 10 problems
MCQ on matrix properties, rank, consistency and eigenvalue computation
NumPy/MATLAB verification of hand-computed results with report

units.title: Complex numbers and Fourier series
units.co: Apply complex number theory and vector calculus in physics and engineering contexts

topics.s: Algebra of complex numbers, modulus-argument form, De Moivre's theorem, nth roots
Cauchy-Riemann equations, harmonic functions, conformal mapping introduction
Contour integrals, Cauchy's integral theorem and formula
Periodic functions, Dirichlet conditions, Euler's formulae, half-range series
Complex Fourier transform, properties, convolution theorem, engineering applications

teaching.d: Argand diagram, unit circle and Euler's formula — animated visualisations
Show how Fourier series decomposes a square wave — play audio examples
Derive formula then immediately apply it to an engineering scenario
Plot Fourier series approximations with increasing terms — observe convergence

assessment.d: Fourier series expansion of 5 periodic waveforms including even/odd functions
Identify analytic functions, compute Fourier coefficients
Complex integration, Fourier series — 1.5 hour section
1-page application report: Fourier analysis in signal processing or image compression

refs.ti: NPTEL — Transforms and Partial Differential Equations

units.title: Ordinary differential equations
units.co: Formulate and solve ordinary differential equations arising in engineering systems

topics.s: Separable, linear, exact and Bernoulli equations; integrating factor method
Homogeneous and non-homogeneous; complementary function and particular integral
Deriving particular integrals for non-homogeneous equations
Definition, properties, inverse Laplace, solving IVPs using Laplace transforms
Spring-mass system, RC circuit, population dynamics, heat conduction

teaching.d: Derive ODE from a physical system (spring-mass, RC circuit) then solve it
30-minute weekly drill: students solve 5 Laplace transform pairs from memory
Groups model a real physical system as an ODE, solve it and present findings
Solve ODE analytically then verify using Python scipy.integrate.odeint

assessment.d: Solve 10 ODEs covering all methods — separable, linear, Bernoulli, Laplace
ODE formulation, solution and interpretation — 2-hour paper
Groups submit a 2-page report modelling and solving an engineering ODE
Multiple choice on Laplace transform properties and inverse transforms

units.title: Quantum mechanics and wave-particle duality
units.co: Explain quantum mechanical principles underlying semiconductor behaviour

topics.s: de Broglie hypothesis, Davisson-Germer experiment, matter waves
Position-momentum uncertainty, energy-time uncertainty, physical significance
Time-dependent and time-independent forms, wave function and probability density
Infinite potential well, quantised energy levels, wave functions, zero-point energy
Tunnel effect, band theory, valence and conduction bands, Fermi level

teaching.d: Use animations to show wavefunction collapse, probability clouds, energy quantisation
Discuss Schrödinger's cat, double-slit experiment — build quantum intuition
Compute de Broglie wavelengths, energy levels for particle in a box
Demonstrate photoelectric effect simulation using PhET — vary frequency, intensity

assessment.d: Compute de Broglie wavelengths, energy quantisation for 8 problems
MCQ on uncertainty principle, wave functions and band theory
Quantum mechanics section — derive, solve and interpret wave equations
5-minute presentation on a quantum phenomenon of student's choice

units.title: Wave optics — interference and diffraction
units.co: Apply wave optics concepts to analyse interference and diffraction phenomena

topics.s: Conditions for interference, Young's double slit experiment, fringe width, coherence
Newton's rings, anti-reflection coatings, colour of thin films
Huygens' principle, single slit diffraction, diffraction grating, resolving power
Types of polarisation, Malus's law, Brewster's angle, applications in LCDs
Rayleigh criterion, resolving power of telescope and microscope

teaching.d: Show Newton's rings, single-slit diffraction pattern using laser pointer in class
Students explore double-slit, single-slit parameters on PhET virtual lab
Derive fringe width formula, then solve 3 numerical problems immediately
Anti-reflection coatings on camera lenses, diffraction in CD reading

assessment.d: Newton's rings experiment — tabulate, plot and derive wavelength of light
Numericals on fringe width, grating, resolving power
Short answer on polarisation types, Brewster's angle and applications

refs.ti: MIT 8.03 Physics III: Vibrations and Waves

units.title: Lasers and optical fibres
units.co: Describe the principles of lasers, optical fibres and their engineering applications

topics.s: Stimulated emission, population inversion, optical pumping, Einstein's A and B coefficients
He-Ne laser, CO₂ laser, semiconductor laser — construction and operation
Industrial cutting/welding, medical surgery, LIDAR, holography, barcode scanners
Total internal reflection, numerical aperture, types of fibres — step-index, graded-index
Attenuation, dispersion, bandwidth, splicing, connectors, FTTH applications

teaching.d: Show stimulated vs spontaneous emission using animation; diagram population inversion
Watch 10-min video on fibre optic cable manufacturing and FTTH deployment
Invite a telecom professional to explain real-world fibre optic networks
Demonstrate laser diffraction, total internal reflection using optical fibre kit

assessment.d: Numericals on numerical aperture, acceptance angle and fibre attenuation
MCQ on types of lasers, population inversion conditions and fibre types
5-minute student presentation on a laser or fibre optic application
Laser and fibre optics questions — 2 units in end-semester paper

units.title: Electromagnetism and electronic devices
units.co: Relate principles of electromagnetism to the working of electronic devices

topics.s: Gauss's law, Faraday's law, Ampere's law, displacement current — integral and differential forms
Wave equation derivation, speed of light, transverse nature, Poynting vector
Dielectric polarisation, permittivity, dielectric breakdown, energy storage
Diamagnetic, paramagnetic, ferromagnetic materials; hysteresis, B-H curve
p-n junction, diode characteristics, Zener diode, transistor operation in CE configuration

teaching.d: Derive Maxwell's equations from physical laws with conceptual explanation at each step
Simulate diode and transistor circuits using Tinkercad or Multisim
Map each Maxwell equation to a real device — antenna, transformer, motor, capacitor
Solve numericals on Poynting vector, dielectric constant, transistor biasing

assessment.d: 10 numericals on Maxwell's equations, dielectrics and semiconductor characteristics
p-n junction and Zener diode characteristic curves — plot and analyse
MCQ on electromagnetic wave properties, magnetic material classification

units.title: Physics experiments and error analysis
units.co: Conduct physics experiments and interpret results with appropriate error analysis

topics.s: SI units, dimensions, dimensional analysis, unit conversion
Systematic, random and gross errors; absolute, relative and percentage error
Rules for error in sum, difference, product, quotient and powers
Mean, median, standard deviation, normal distribution, chi-squared test
Structure of a physics lab report, tabulation, graphs, conclusions, referencing

teaching.d: 6 mandatory experiments: Newton's rings, laser diffraction, Zener, B-H curve, torsional pendulum, Lee's disc
Given a dataset, students compute mean, SD, propagated error and write conclusions
Students exchange lab reports and provide structured feedback using a rubric
Manual and Python (matplotlib) plotting of experimental data with error bars

assessment.d: All 6 experiments with readings, graphs, calculations and conclusions
Lab viva at end of semester — questions on procedure, sources of error, significance
Formal 2-page report on a given dataset with full error propagation analysis
Questions on error analysis, dimensional analysis, and measurement

units.title: Foundations of technical communication
units.co: Produce clear and well-structured technical documents including reports and manuals

topics.s: Sender-receiver model, types of communication, noise, feedback loops
Characteristics of technical style — precision, clarity, objectivity, brevity
Visual hierarchy, use of headings, white space, fonts, numbered lists
Grammar review — subject-verb agreement, tense consistency, pronoun reference
Avoiding jargon, active vs passive voice, nominalisations, sentence length

teaching.d: Students rewrite 5 poorly worded technical sentences and discuss improvements
Analyse a real technical manual extract — identify good and bad practices
Show before/after examples of unclear vs clear technical writing
Exchange written paragraphs and mark up issues using a checklist

assessment.d: Rewrite 10 vague or poorly written technical statements in clear style
1-page written critique of a given technical document — structure, style, clarity
Grammar, sentence rewriting, document design questions
Identify passive constructions, nominalisation and ambiguous pronoun reference

units.title: Oral communication and professional presentations
units.co: Demonstrate effective oral communication skills through presentations and group discussions

topics.s: Opening, body, conclusion; rule of three; signposting; slide design principles
Voice modulation, eye contact, pace, gestures, managing nervousness
Roles in a GD, listening skills, building on others' ideas, consensus building
STAR method for answering questions, elevator pitch, professional vocabulary
Effective use of slides, whiteboards, demos; avoiding death by PowerPoint

teaching.d: Each student delivers a 3-minute prepared presentation on a technical topic
Students receive a topic 2 minutes before speaking for 90 seconds — develops fluency
Class conducts a structured GD on a technology ethics topic with peer evaluation
Students watch recording of their own presentation and self-evaluate against rubric

assessment.d: 5-minute technical presentation with Q&A — assessed on content, delivery, slides
Assessed GD participation — contribution, clarity, listening, leadership
Students evaluate 2 peer presentations using a structured rubric
500-word written reflection on personal communication strengths and areas to improve

units.title: Technical report and documentation writing
units.co: Apply grammar, style and precision standards to professional engineering writing

topics.s: Formal report structure — title, abstract, ToC, introduction, body, conclusion, references; lab reports, feasibility reports
Informative vs descriptive abstract, word limits, what to include/exclude
Numbered steps, warnings, safety notes, audience adaptation, testing instructions
IEEE citation style, in-text citations, reference list format, plagiarism awareness
Systematic editing checklist, common grammar pitfalls, spell-check limitations

teaching.d: Students draft an abstract and introduction section of a sample lab report in class
Given 5 sources, students format full IEEE citations and check each other's work
Students receive a 1-page report with 15 planted errors and must identify and correct all
Demonstrate what constitutes plagiarism; discuss paraphrasing vs copying

assessment.d: Full formal report on a lab experiment — structure, abstract, analysis, references
Write a step-by-step manual for a simple software or hardware procedure
Format 8 references in IEEE style and cite them correctly in a paragraph
Identify and correct grammar, citation and structure errors in a given document

units.title: Critical reading and argumentation
units.co: Construct logical arguments and critical responses to technical and professional scenarios

topics.s: SQ3R method, identifying main claims, assumptions and evidence in technical texts
Claim-evidence-warrant model, deductive vs inductive reasoning, logical fallacies
Summary, critique and synthesis — how to respond critically to a technical article
Problem-solution structure, recommendations, call to action, hedging language
Professional email format, subject lines, tone, response time, avoiding misinterpretation

teaching.d: Students annotate a 2-page technical article, identifying claims, evidence and assumptions
Write a 300-word critical response to a technology opinion piece
Structured academic debate — AI replacing jobs, data privacy, open source software
Students receive 5 scenarios and write appropriate professional emails for each

assessment.d: 500-word critical response to an assigned technical article — argument, evidence, style
1-page written proposal recommending a solution to a campus tech problem
Write 3 professional emails from given scenarios under 30-minute time limit
Comprehension, argument analysis and short writing task

units.title: Collaborative communication and professional ethics
units.co: Collaborate ethically and respectfully in diverse team communication settings

topics.s: Roles in collaborative writing, version control for documents, shared editing tools
High vs low context cultures, tone differences, global professional norms
Honesty, transparency, confidentiality, intellectual property in professional contexts
Active listening, empathy, de-escalation language, negotiation strategies
LinkedIn profiles, professional messaging, social media in the workplace

teaching.d: Groups of 4 co-write a 3-page technical report using Google Docs — tracked changes assessed
Analyse 3 real engineering communication ethics cases — whistleblowing, misrepresentation
Simulate a difficult professional conversation — giving bad news, conflict, apology
Students set up or improve their LinkedIn profile with peer feedback

assessment.d: Co-authored 3-page technical report — assessed individually on contribution and quality
500-word reflection on an ethical communication dilemma from engineering practice
Peer and instructor review of professional profile — completeness, tone, accuracy
Cross-cultural scenarios, ethics questions, professional email correction

units.title: Python basics — I/O, data types and operators
units.co: Implement Python programs to solve problems involving sequences, strings and files

topics.m: Lab 1: Environment setup and first programs
topics.s: Install Python, set up VS Code, run Hello World, use print() and input()

topics.m: Lab 2: Variables, data types and operators
topics.s: int, float, str, bool; arithmetic, relational, logical operators; type casting
String indexing, slicing, concatenation, repetition, built-in string methods

topics.m: Lab 4: Input validation and formatted output
topics.s: int(input()), exception basics, f-strings, format specifiers
Compute BMI, temperature converter, simple interest calculator using input/output

teaching.d: Students code at workstations with real-time instructor support and guidance
Students work in pairs — driver writes code, navigator reviews — switch roles each exercise
Instructor intentionally introduces errors and walks through debugging process
5 graded problems per session submitted at the end of each lab

assessment.d: Completed and verified programs for all Unit 1 exercises with sample outputs
Write 2 programs in 1 hour from problem statements — string manipulation and I/O
Explain code logic, variable types and operator precedence in a short oral session
Weekly lab exercise sheet — 5 programs per week

refs.ti: Python Programming: An Introduction to Computer Science
Automate the Boring Stuff with Python

units.title: Control structures and functions
units.co: Write modular programs using functions, recursion and exception handling

topics.s: if-elif-else chains, nested conditions, ternary operator, truth tables
for loop, while loop, nested loops, break, continue, pass, loop-else
def, parameters, return, default arguments, keyword arguments, global vs local scope
Recursive factorial, Fibonacci, sum of digits, tower of Hanoi — trace the call stack
try-except-else-finally, raising exceptions, custom exceptions, input error handling

teaching.d: Given printed code, students trace execution and predict output before running
Start with a stub function, gradually add logic, test at each step
Extension problems for fast-finishers — FizzBuzz variants, Pascal's triangle
Teams race to fix a buggy program — first correct submission wins

assessment.d: All Unit 2 programs with correct outputs, neatly formatted
Write a function-based program with exception handling — 1.5 hours
Student submits their recursive solution and reviews a peer's solution
Explain recursion trace and exception handling logic for one selected program

refs.ti: Python Programming: An Introduction to Computer Science

units.title: Object-oriented programming in Python
units.co: Apply object-oriented concepts by building simple class-based Python programs

topics.s: class keyword, __init__, self, instance variables, creating objects, calling methods
Single and multi-level inheritance, method overriding, super(), isinstance()
Private attributes, getters/setters, __str__, __repr__, __len__, __eq__
Design and implement a BankAccount, Library or Student Management system using OOP
Build a small command-line application using at least 3 classes with inheritance

teaching.d: Instructor builds a Vehicle → Car → ElectricCar hierarchy live, explaining each step
Students sketch a class diagram on paper before writing code — validates design thinking
Pairs design and implement a system together — one handles base class, other handles subclass
Students present their OOP design choices to the class for feedback

assessment.d: All OOP programs with class diagrams, outputs and comments
Functional CLI application using OOP — assessed on design, functionality and code quality
Implement a given OOP problem — class hierarchy, methods, inheritance — 1.5 hours
Explain class design, inheritance structure and method overriding choices

refs.ti: Python Programming: An Introduction to Computer Science

units.title: Testing, debugging and best practices
units.co: Test and debug programs systematically using test cases and debugging tools

topics.s: Breakpoints, step-through, watch variables, call stack inspection
Boundary values, equivalence partitioning, edge cases, testing a sorting function
Installing pytest, writing test functions, assert statements, running tests, fixtures
timeit module, cProfile, identifying bottlenecks, optimising a slow loop
pylint, flake8, Black formatter — run on existing code and fix all warnings

teaching.d: Students receive a program with 5 hidden bugs and must find all within 30 minutes
Write tests first, then write code to pass them — reverse of typical approach
Instructor profiles an inefficient sort; students optimise and compare runtimes
Run pylint on previous assignments and fix all warnings as a refactoring exercise

assessment.d: Find and fix all bugs in 3 given programs — document each bug and fix
Write a full pytest test suite for a provided function library — min 10 tests
Given a buggy program and requirements — debug, test and optimise in 1.5 hours
Explain testing strategy, boundary values chosen and profiling findings

units.title: Capstone mini project
units.co: Develop a mini project demonstrating end-to-end problem solving in Python

topics.s: Choose a problem, define requirements, create a simple project plan and timeline
Identify classes, functions, data structures; sketch flowchart and data flow
Build the project in stages — core features first, then enhancements; commit to GitHub
Write test cases, fix bugs, run linter, write docstrings for all functions
Live demo to peers, README on GitHub, viva with instructor

teaching.d: Instructor reviews each project proposal and provides written feedback before coding starts
10-minute weekly progress check with instructor — what's done, what's next, what's blocked
Each student demos their project to 3 peers who complete a structured feedback form
Students walk the instructor through their code explaining design decisions

assessment.d: 1-page proposal with problem statement, features list and GitHub repo link
Final project code with commit history, README, docstrings and test files
10-minute live demonstration of all features to instructor and peers
Instructor Q&A on code design, data structures used, challenges faced and lessons learned

refs.ti: Python Programming: An Introduction to Computer Science
Automate the Boring Stuff with Python
CS50P — Introduction to Programming with Python

units.title: Logic gates and Boolean algebra
units.co: Verify the truth tables of basic and universal logic gates through circuit implementation

topics.s: AND, OR, NOT — verify truth tables using IC 7408, 7432, 7404
NAND and NOR as universal gates — implement AND, OR, NOT using only NAND
Implement and verify XOR and XNOR; observe parity property
Implement a 3-variable Boolean expression and verify with truth table
Introduce Logisim tool — draw and simulate all gates, verify digitally before hardware

teaching.d: 5-minute quiz before each lab to verify students understood the concept
Instructor demonstrates first circuit; students replicate and extend
Students build in Logisim first, then on breadboard — compare results
Students fill in observation tables and draw circuit diagrams in real time

assessment.d: Verified truth tables, circuit diagrams and observations for all 5 labs
Given a Boolean expression — implement it on breadboard within 30 minutes
Explain universal gate property and show implementation for any gate using NAND only
5-question quiz before each lab — 5 quizzes total

units.title: Combinational circuit design
units.co: Design and test combinational circuits including adders, multiplexers and decoders

topics.m: Lab 6: Half adder and full adder
topics.s: Design using basic gates; implement with IC 7486 (XOR) and verify
Cascade full adders; verify sum and carry outputs for all input combinations
Implement 4:1 MUX using IC 74153; use as universal logic element
3:8 decoder using IC 74138; implement Boolean functions using decoder
Design a 1-bit comparator; extend to 4-bit using IC 7485

teaching.d: Students design the circuit on paper and get it verified before picking up ICs
Instructor models IC pin checking, continuity testing, logic probe use
Full Logisim simulation required as prerequisite to hardware build
Introduce common wiring mistakes; students identify faults in a prepared faulty circuit

assessment.d: Design sheets, circuit diagrams, truth table verification for all 5 labs
Design and implement a given combinational circuit — adder or decoder — in 45 minutes
Given a problem statement, design a combinational circuit using Logisim and submit
Explain MUX as universal logic element and full adder carry propagation

units.title: Sequential circuits — flip-flops and registers
units.co: Implement and analyse sequential circuits including flip-flops, registers and counters

topics.s: Implement SR latch using NAND gates; build D flip-flop using IC 7474
Verify JK flip-flop truth table using IC 7476; observe race-around condition
4-bit SISO, SIPO, PISO using IC 74194 — verify serial and parallel modes
Design and verify 4-bit asynchronous counter using JK flip-flops and IC 7493
Implement MOD-N counter using IC 74163; design custom sequence counter

teaching.d: Draw state diagram on board before each sequential circuit lab — relate to hardware
Use oscilloscope or LED blinking to visualise clock edges and flip-flop state changes
Students draw timing diagrams from given input sequences before verifying on hardware
Students locate pin diagrams and timing specifications from official IC datasheets

assessment.d: State diagrams, timing diagrams, observations and truth tables for all 5 labs
Design a MOD-N counter or shift register circuit and implement on breadboard
Draw timing diagrams for a 3-stage counter given the clock waveform
Explain race-around condition in JK flip-flops and how master-slave design resolves it

units.title: Digital simulation and EDA tools
units.co: Use simulation tools to model and test digital circuits before hardware implementation

topics.s: Build a 4-bit ALU in Logisim — add, subtract, AND, OR operations with control lines
Module structure, wire/reg, assign, always block — simulate a half adder in Verilog
Implement 4:1 MUX and 3:8 decoder in Verilog; simulate using ModelSim or EDA Playground
Code a D flip-flop and 4-bit counter in Verilog with testbench
Design and simulate a complete 4-bit calculator or traffic light controller in Verilog
